free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of protons by 7Li
The target,isotopically enriched 7Li(99.97%) evaporated
on a thin Formvar film was less than 10 keV thick for
5 MeV protons, including the backing
Silicon, surface- barrier detector
Total error in the absolute cross sections.
Two angular distributions at 4.2 and 8.0 MeV were used
to normalized the excitation curves and to get relative
cross sections. The absolute cross section scale was
chosen so as to get the best overlap with the
low-energy data of Malmberg and of Bashkin. (MONIT-REF)
Compilation Produced by RFNC-VNIIEF Sarov
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: ref. in MONIT-REF corrected:
J,PR,86 -> J,PR,84. ERR-ANALYS corrected.
SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code in Subent 002-004.
Titles of digitizing errors changed according to Dict.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
The error in the elastic cross section
caused by statistic and background subtraction is
between 2% and 4% at the lower energies and rises to
about 8% at the highest energies.
Fig 2a from Nucl.Phys.,63(1965)650

presentation on fig.
SD: Free text updated. DATA-ERR -> ERR-1.
Typical errors in the inelastic cross
section caused by statistics and background subtraction
is between 5% and 7%.
